Forest Name: Pike and San Isabel National Forests
Area Within District: Barr trail, TH in Manitou Springs at the Cog Railway Depot
Location Details: Begin the collection hike from the TH at the Cog railway parking lot. Park in town to avoid parking fees. Take the free shuttle to the TH. It comes every 20 minutes. The first mile of the Barr Trail is exposed foothills. In mile 2 and 3, to the top of the Incline, the P. virens is continuous along the trail in the P. ponderosa woodlands. Collection can occur while hiking the trail. Near to the top, there are large patches of P. virens, almost in monoculture. A good approach would be to walk to the top and collect on the way down. The amount of trailside collection can be determined by how much seed is obtained from the large patches near the top of this section.
Creator: cnhp_guest4
Notes on Associated Species: Geranium caespitosum, Yucca glauca, Allium cernuum, Quercus gambellii, Pseudotsuga menziesii, Holodiscus discolor, Penstemon barbatus, Solidago rigida, Ribes cereum
